verse_specific_anchors: The explicit RANKING — 'far below' / 'dūreṇa avaram' — is verse-specific to 2.49. 2.47 names the structure (right to action, not to fruits) but does not rank stances; 2.50 defines yoga as kauśalam in action. 2.49 alone uses dūreṇa to comparatively rank fruit-driven action below buddhi-yoga.

The kṛpaṇa tag — 'pitiable' / 'miserly' — is verse-specific. 2.47 calls fruit-attached action wrong; 2.49 calls fruit-attached actors kṛpaṇāḥ. The Engineer B framing in the analog ('not failing, pitiable') is the verse's specific tonal contribution.

The śaraṇam (refuge) move — 'buddhau śaraṇam anviccha' — is verse-specific. 2.47 does not use the refuge grammar; 18.66 will use it terminally. 2.49 introduces the early-chapter refuge-in-buddhi grammar.
verdict_reason: Some overlap with 2.47 and 2.50 is unavoidable because the three verses are part of a single argument (2.47-2.51 is the buddhi-yoga sequence). The analog's specific anchors — explicit ranking, kṛpaṇa-tagging, refuge-in-buddhi — are 2.49's contributions, not 2.47's or 2.50's. An informed reader given the analog and asked which of 2.47/2.49/2.50/2.51 it adapts would converge on 2.49 because of the 'far below' / 'pitiable' / 'refuge in buddhi' triad. Pass.
